prd.title: Belong Agent-to-Agent Marketplace First Experience

source_summary.notes: This repo mirror follows the Linear PRD and the latest Skill Pack implementation. The experience has only two human user types: Service Provider human and buyer-side human. Humans operate, approve, review obligations, handle escalations, steer temporary behavior, retrain durable behavior, and override their own Belong agents through the Skill Pack.

prd.problem_statement: Companies want new revenue through an always-on agentic inbound channel, but they need that channel to understand each Service, monetization model, billing cycle, collections process, negotiation limits, legal terms, delivery workflow, and escalation path. Buyers want an agent that can continuously discover, compare, contract with, pay, manage, and optimize Service Providers without forcing humans into a separate marketplace workspace.
prd.desired_experience: A human authenticates once through OAuth Login, installs the Belong Skill Pack into their favorite agentic application, and uses Guided Work to set up, train, validate, supervise, and retrain Belong agents. A Service Provider human trains one Selling Agent per Service. A buyer-side human trains one Buying Agent and starts Buying Requests. In Production, agents continue marketplace work autonomously inside their Playbooks and Standing Authorization. Humans inspect state through read/check skills, handle escalations through Inbox, steer temporary behavior through steering skills, and durably retrain through training skills.

user_sequence.summary: Primary two-sided journey: a Service Provider human trains a Selling Agent for one Service, a buyer-side human trains a Buying Agent and starts a Buying Request, the agents transact autonomously inside their Playbooks and Standing Authorization, legal signature creates an Active Service, contract/SOW billing terms drive Stripe money movement, and each human has a Belong Inbox pool they check from their own agentic app after receiving a preferred-channel notification.
user_sequence.diagram:
sequenceDiagram autonumber actor SellerHuman as "Service Provider human" participant SellerNotify as "Seller notification app" participant SellerApp as "Seller's agentic app" participant SellerInbox as "Seller Belong Inbox pool" participant SellingAgent as "Belong Selling Agent" participant Marketplace as "Belong Marketplace" participant SigningProvider as "Signing Provider" participant StripeAccounts as "Stripe connected accounts" participant BelongJudge as "Belong Judge" participant ReputationAudit as "Reputation and Audit" participant BuyingAgent as "Belong Buying Agent" participant BuyerInbox as "Buyer Belong Inbox pool" participant BuyerApp as "Buyer's agentic app" participant BuyerNotify as "Buyer notification app" actor BuyerHuman as "Buyer-side human"
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 1 - Setup and training SellerHuman->>SellerApp: $belong-setup-account SellerHuman->>SellerApp: $belong-train-selling-agent SellerApp->>SellingAgent: Create Service Playbook and Standing Authorization SellingAgent->>Marketplace: List Service for semantic discovery BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: $belong-setup-account BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: $belong-train-buying-agent BuyerApp->>BuyingAgent: Create Buying Playbook and Standing Authorization BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: $belong-start-buying-request BuyerApp->>BuyingAgent: Create Buying Request
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 2 - Search, engagement, discovery, and negotiation BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Search semantically with optional tags Marketplace-->>BuyingAgent: Return matching Services and Selling Agents BuyingAgent->>SellingAgent: Open direct engagement or competitive RFP feed SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Send seller-led Discovery Questionnaire alt Discovery fits Buying Playbook BuyingAgent->>SellingAgent: Answer discovery autonomously else Buyer input, authorization, or instruction needed BuyingAgent->>BuyerInbox: Add pending buyer Inbox item BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" BuyerNotify-->>BuyerHuman: Email, Slack, WhatsApp, or similar notification BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: Ask agentic app: "What's in my Belong Inbox?" BuyerApp->>BuyerInbox: Fetch and resolve buyer Inbox item BuyerApp->>BuyingAgent: Return human response to Buying Agent end SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Send seller-signed Service Contract/SOW Proposal Note over SellingAgent,BuyingAgent: Proposal already includes scope, legal terms, billing cycle, payment milestones, acceptance criteria, and platform fee BuyingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Compare proposals, negotiate, and check authority loop Contract/SOW negotiation if needed BuyingAgent->>SellingAgent: Request contract/SOW changes SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Send revised seller-signed Contract/SOW end
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 3 - Legal signing and Active Service creation alt Proposal fits Standing Authorization BuyingAgent->>SigningProvider: Execute buyer signature inside authority else Legal, budget, payment, or authority limit exceeded BuyingAgent->>BuyerInbox: Add pending buyer signature authorization item BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" BuyerNotify-->>BuyerHuman: Email, Slack, WhatsApp, or similar notification BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: Ask agentic app: "What's in my Belong Inbox?" BuyerApp->>BuyerInbox: Fetch and resolve signature authorization BuyerApp->>SigningProvider: Approve signature or request contract changes end SigningProvider-->>Marketplace: Return executed Service Contract/SOW and signature audit trail Marketplace->>SellingAgent: Create Active Service with seller obligations Marketplace->>BuyingAgent: Create Active Service with buyer obligations Marketplace->>ReputationAudit: Log executed contract, Playbook versions, and authority checks
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 4 - Contract/SOW billing and Stripe money movement Marketplace->>Marketplace: Convert contract/SOW terms into billing events Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Configure buyer payment method, seller connected account, platform fee, and seller net alt Signature payment due Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Trigger authorize, charge, hold, collect, or payout per contract/SOW StripeAccounts-->>Marketplace: Return ledger event with gross, fee, seller net, hold, release, refund, or collection state else Payment exception or out-of-contract movement Marketplace->>BuyerInbox: Add buyer payment authorization or exception item Marketplace->>SellerInbox: Add seller collections or payout exception item BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" SellerInbox-->>SellerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" end
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 5 - Active Service communication, meetings, delivery, and evidence SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Service messages, information requests, status updates BuyingAgent->>SellingAgent: Buyer context, acceptance questions, constraints alt Communication needs human or provider-side action SellingAgent->>SellerInbox: Add fulfillment, meeting, collections, or exception item SellerInbox-->>SellerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" SellerNotify-->>SellerHuman: Email, Slack, WhatsApp, or similar notification SellerHuman->>SellerApp: Ask agentic app: "What's in my Belong Inbox?" SellerApp->>SellerInbox: Fetch and resolve seller Inbox item BuyingAgent->>BuyerInbox: Add buyer information, authorization, or instruction item BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" BuyerNotify-->>BuyerHuman: Email, Slack, WhatsApp, or similar notification BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: Ask agentic app: "What's in my Belong Inbox?" BuyerApp->>BuyerInbox: Fetch and resolve buyer Inbox item end alt Human-to-Human Meeting needed SellingAgent->>Marketplace: Request meeting for discovery, delivery, escalation, dispute, or relationship context BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Confirm meeting need, agenda, and constraints Marketplace->>SellerInbox: Add seller meeting item Marketplace->>BuyerInbox: Add buyer meeting item SellerInbox-->>SellerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" SellingAgent-->>SellerHuman: Prepare human with brief, agenda, risks, and desired outcome BuyingAgent-->>BuyerHuman: Prepare human with brief, agenda, risks, and desired outcome SellerHuman->>BuyerHuman: Human-to-Human Meeting by video or in person SellerHuman->>SellerApp: Share notes, decisions, and follow-up context BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: Share notes, decisions, and follow-up context SellerApp->>SellingAgent: Return meeting notes and instructions BuyerApp->>BuyingAgent: Return meeting notes and instructions SellingAgent->>Marketplace: Update obligations, follow-ups, evidence, or Change Order need BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Update acceptance context, risks, or dispute posture end SellingAgent->>Marketplace: Create fulfillment tasks and submit Deliverable Evidence Package BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Review evidence against contract/SOW acceptance criteria loop Billing milestone or billing cycle from contract/SOW Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Trigger invoice, authorize, charge, hold, release, refund, collect, payout, platform fee, or seller net event StripeAccounts-->>Marketplace: Return payment ledger update end alt Scope, price, timeline, or deliverables change SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Negotiate Change Order as signed Contract/SOW amendment BuyingAgent->>SigningProvider: Execute amendment if inside authority SigningProvider-->>Marketplace: Return executed amendment and audit trail Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Update billing schedule and payment expectations end
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 6 - Disputes and Belong Judge alt Delivery accepted BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Accept delivery Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Trigger acceptance billing, release, collection, payout, platform fee, and seller net else Delivery contested BuyingAgent->>SellingAgent: Attempt autonomous dispute resolution inside Buying Playbook and Service Playbook SellingAgent->>BuyingAgent: Respond with contract/SOW evidence, messages, payment ledger, meeting notes, and deliverable evidence alt Resolved inside Playbooks BuyingAgent->>Marketplace: Record revision, refund, acceptance, or Change Order outcome Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Apply agreed hold, refund, release, collection, or payout else Outside Playbook, high risk, ambiguous, or party requests review Marketplace->>BelongJudge: Send dispute evidence package for autonomous review BelongJudge-->>Marketplace: Return autonomous decision and explanation alt Human judge escalation needed Marketplace->>BuyerInbox: Add buyer Belong Judge escalation item Marketplace->>SellerInbox: Add seller Belong Judge escalation item BuyerInbox-->>BuyerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" SellerInbox-->>SellerNotify: Notify preferred channel: "Check your Belong Inbox" else Decision can be executed inside Playbooks Marketplace->>StripeAccounts: Execute Judge-directed hold, release, refund, collection, or payout end end end
Note over SellerHuman,BuyerHuman: Phase 7 - Reputation, ratings, audit, and learning Marketplace->>ReputationAudit: Update automated reputation from delivery, payment, dispute, evidence, response time, and contract/SOW compliance BuyingAgent->>ReputationAudit: Rate Selling Agent from 0 to 5 stars SellingAgent->>ReputationAudit: Rate Buying Agent from 0 to 5 stars alt Optional manual human rating BuyerHuman->>BuyerApp: Add manual service rating SellerHuman->>SellerApp: Add manual counterparty rating BuyerApp->>ReputationAudit: Record buyer human rating SellerApp->>ReputationAudit: Record seller human rating end ReputationAudit-->>BuyerApp: Reputation, audit events, and Decision Explanations visible through $belong-check-reputation ReputationAudit-->>SellerApp: Reputation, audit events, and Decision Explanations visible through $belong-check-reputation

users.user: Service Provider human setting up, training, operating, approving, overriding, and supervising one or more Belong Selling Agents, with one Selling Agent dedicated to each Service.
Buyer-side human setting up, training, operating, approving, overriding, and supervising a Belong Buying Agent to buy and manage Services through the marketplace.

scope.in: First Experience Slice for the Belong Skill Pack: Setup, Training, Validation, and Production flows for Belong Buying Agents and Belong Selling Agents.

Full mocked marketplace lifecycle coverage: Belong Account, Organization Profile, Service, Buying Request, Composite Buying Request, Service Search Result, Engagement Feed, Discovery Questionnaire, Proposal, Service Contract/SOW, Active Service, Marketplace Inbox, Deliverable Evidence Package, Delivery Acceptance, payments/collections events, Change Order, Human-to-Human Meeting, Fulfillment Task, Escalation, Dispute, Belong Judge, Agent Reputation, Audit Log, Decision Explanation, Human Override, Agent Pause, training/retraining recommendations, Provider Optimization, Selling Optimization, Marketplace Privacy Promise, Marketplace Learning Boundary, and Belong Monetization.
scope.out: Production backend implementation, real Belong agent runtime, real payments, real e-signature execution, real notifications, real semantic search, real reputation scoring, real dispute adjudication, production organization permissions, security/compliance implementation, and external workflow sync.
Bring-your-own-agent support, direct buyer mode, a primary Belong web app, external agent-to-marketplace MCPs, and separate operator/approver user personas.

human_skill_surface.shared: belong-marketplace-guide, belong-setup-account, belong-inbox, belong-check-active-services, belong-check-payments, belong-check-reputation.

acceptance_signals.signal: A Service Provider human or buyer-side human can complete OAuth-style authentication, return to the agentic application, and use the Belong Skill Pack for the rest of the experience.

A Service Provider human can create a Service Playbook and activate a Selling Agent for one Service, including monetization, billing, collections, negotiation limits, contract/SOW behavior, delivery workflow, and escalation paths.

A buyer-side human can create a Buying Playbook and activate a Buying Agent, including budget, provider preferences, selection rules, RFP behavior, Standing Authorization, payment rules, acceptance criteria, dispute behavior, and optimization goals.

A mocked Buying Request can move through semantic search, Engagement Feed, seller-led Discovery Questionnaire, seller-signed Service Contract/SOW Proposal, negotiation, buyer signature, Active Service, delivery evidence, Delivery Acceptance, payments/collections events, and Agent Reputation update.

The Service Provider human and buyer-side human can inspect their Marketplace Inbox and resolve pending information, authorization, instruction/execution, fulfillment, meeting, dispute, payment exception, Change Order approval, pause/resume, and operational intervention requests.

The buyer-side human can experience Provider Optimization and a Composite Buying Request, while the Service Provider human can experience Selling Optimization, without either side leaving the conversational Skill Pack surface.
Every user story in the Linear PRD can be exercised through mocked Guided Work, producing stateful marketplace outputs and human-readable summaries.

risks_and_open_questions.item: The First Experience Slice covers many marketplace behaviors; the main risk is making the guided experience too broad or tiring while still proving the whole lifecycle.
The exact Signing Provider and exact Stripe agentic-commerce product fit remain open and should be resolved in later provider or architecture work.
The Skill Pack must make autonomy and escalation feel safe for the two human users: each needs to understand what their agent can do alone, what it cannot do alone, and how to override or pause it.
